heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by François Truffaut. Written by François Truffaut and Marcel Moussy.
clean: Directed by François Truffaut. Written by François Truffaut and Marcel Moussy.

heading: Julien Doinel
raw: I have no socks left around these holes.
clean: I have no socks left around these holes.

heading: Petite Feuille
raw: Now, Doinel, go get some water and erase those insanities, or I'll make you lick the wall, my friend.
clean: Now, Doinel, go get some water and erase those insanities, or I'll make you lick the wall, my friend.

heading: Petite Feuille
raw: Doinel, if your paper is first today, it's because I've decided to give the results beginning with the worst.
clean: Doinel, if your paper is first today, it's because I've decided to give the results beginning with the worst.

heading: Juvenile Delinquent
raw: Every time I cried, my father would imitate me on his fiddle, just to drive me nuts. One day I got fed up and I knocked him out.
clean: Every time I cried, my father would imitate me on his fiddle, just to drive me nuts. One day I got fed up and I knocked him out.

heading: Juvenile Delinquent
raw: [to Antoine about another boy in the reformatory] He escaped a week ago, but they got him. Around here to escape is bad enough, but getting caught is worse.
clean: He escaped a week ago, but they got him. Around here to escape is bad enough, but getting caught is worse.

heading: Juvenile Delinquent
raw: If she drops her pen, pick it up, but don't look at her legs. Or else it will be on your record.
clean: If she drops her pen, pick it up, but don't look at her legs. Or else it will be on your record.

heading: Juvenile Delinquent
raw: I'm an unstable psychotic individual with perverted tendencies.
clean: I'm an unstable psychotic individual with perverted tendencies.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gilberte Doinel: I'm not in the mood.
clean: I'm not in the mood.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Julien Doinel: Too bad, I am.
clean: Too bad, I am.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoine Doinel: I need some money for lunch, dad. Only 1,000 francs.
clean: I need some money for lunch, dad. Only 1,000 francs.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Julien Doinel: Therefore you hope for 500. Therefore you need 300. Here's 100.
clean: Therefore you hope for 500. Therefore you need 300. Here's 100.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Gilberte Doinel: The best thing is to eat out until the end of the month.
clean: The best thing is to eat out until the end of the month.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Julien Doinel: For that, I need a clean shirt.
clean: For that, I need a clean shirt.

heading: Dialogue
raw: English Teacher: Last and simple question. Where is the father?
clean: Last and simple question. Where is the father?

heading: Dialogue
raw: René: Ze fazer...
clean: René: Ze fazer...

heading: Dialogue
raw: English Teacher: No. The father.
clean: No. The father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: René: Ze fazer.
clean: René: Ze fazer.

heading: Dialogue
raw: English Teacher: No, the tip of the tongue between the teeth. As if you had a lisp. Father.
clean: No, the tip of the tongue between the teeth. As if you had a lisp. Father.

heading: Dialogue
raw: René: Fazer.
clean: René: Fazer.

heading: Dialogue
raw: René: But I can't, sir. Not everybody has a tongue like yours.
clean: René: But I can't, sir. Not everybody has a tongue like yours.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Psychiatrist: Your parents say you're always lying.
clean: Your parents say you're always lying.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Antoine Doinel: Oh, I lie now and then, I suppose. Sometimes I'd tell them the truth and they still wouldn't believe me, so I prefer to lie.
clean: Oh, I lie now and then, I suppose. Sometimes I'd tell them the truth and they still wouldn't believe me, so I prefer to lie.
